limited CNS-penetrant EGFR-TKIs available", "significance": "First comparative evidence of osimertinib CNS efficacy vs chemotherapy in T790M-positive patients from a phase III trial", "regulatory_impact": "Supports CNS activity as key differentiator for osimertinib in T790M-positive disease"}, "potential_applications": {"clinical_utility": ["First-line consideration for T790M-positive patients with existing or high risk of CNS metastases", "Preferred treatment over platinum-based chemotherapy for CNS disease control", "May reduce need for whole-brain radiation in select patients"], "treatment_sequencing": "Positioning osimertinib before chemotherapy for better CNS outcomes", "future_directions": ["Comparative studies vs other EGFR-TKIs with CNS activity", "Exploration in leptomeningeal disease", "Combination strategies with radiation"], "patient_selection": "Routine CNS imaging recommended for T790M-positive patients to identify candidates for osimertinib"}, "limitations": ["Pre-planned subgroup analysis rather than primary study endpoint", "Small sample size in measurable lesion subgroup (n=46)", "Excluded patients with symptomatic or unstable CNS metastases", "Single-agent osimertinib; no combination data"], "conclusion": "Osimertinib demonstrated statistically significant and clinically meaningful superior CNS efficacy compared to platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in T790M-positive advanced NSCLC patients with CNS metastases, with higher ORR (70% vs 31% in measurable lesions), longer duration of response, and significantly longer CNS PFS (11.7 vs 5.6 months, HR 0.32)."}

Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).
Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).

Research: This phase III randomized trial (AURA3) evaluated the central nervous system (CNS) efficacy of osimertinib versus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who had disease progression after prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) treatment. The study enrolled 419 patients, with 116 having measurable and/or nonmeasurable CNS lesions at baseline. Osimertinib demonstrated statistically significant superiority in CNS objective response rate (ORR), duration of response, and progression-free survival compared to platinum-pemetrexed.
Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial

Research: This phase III randomized trial (AURA3) demonstrated superior CNS efficacy of osimertinib versus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in EGFR T790M-positive advanced NSCLC patients with brain metastases. Of 419 randomized patients, 116 had measurable and/or nonmeasurable CNS lesions. Osimertinib achieved a 70% CNS objective response rate (ORR) in patients with measurable lesions versus 31% for chemotherapy (odds ratio 5.13, P=0.015). In the broader CNS evaluable set, ORR was 40% versus 17% (OR 3.24, P=0.014). Median CNS duration of response was 8.9 months vs 5.7 months, and median CNS progression-free survival was 11.7 months vs 5.6 months (HR 0.32, P=0.004) in favor of osimertinib.

Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).
Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).

Research: The AURA3 trial provides the first comparative evidence demonstrating that osimertinib has superior CNS efficacy compared to platinum-pemetrexed in T790M-positive advanced NSCLC patients with CNS metastases. Osimertinib achieved a 70% CNS ORR versus 31% for chemotherapy in patients with measurable lesions, with a hazard ratio of 0.32 for CNS progression-free survival. These findings establish osimertinib as the standard of care for EGFR T790M-positive patients with CNS involvement, addressing a critical unmet need for targeted therapies with effective CNS penetration.

Research: The AURA3 phase III trial demonstrated superior CNS efficacy of osimertinib versus platinum-pemetrexed in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced NSCLC and CNS metastases. In patients with measurable CNS lesions, osimertinib achieved a 70% objective response rate compared to 31% for platinum-pemetrexed (OR 5.13, p=0.015). For all patients with CNS lesions (measurable and nonmeasurable), the ORR was 40% vs 17% (OR 3.24, p=0.014). Median CNS progression-free survival was significantly longer with osimertinib (11.7 months vs 5.6 months; HR 0.32, p=0.004), and median duration of response was 8.9 months versus 5.7 months.
Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial
Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial

Research: This phase III randomized trial (AURA3) evaluated the central nervous system (CNS) efficacy of osimertinib versus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who had progressed on prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy. The study enrolled 419 patients, of whom 116 had measurable and/or nonmeasurable CNS lesions. Osimertinib demonstrated statistically significant superior CNS activity across all endpoints, including objective response rate (ORR), duration of response, and progression-free survival. These findings establish osimertinib as the first EGFR-TKI with comparative CNS efficacy data from a randomized phase III trial.
